PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-11-2023 Continued To Feb-08-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom: Christopher Jew, Esq. for Sang Su (Petitioner); Mimi Su and Hing Su (Objectors/Respondents). Sang Su and Mimi Su were present via Zoom. John Chow, Esq. for Muoi Nam Au and Phat Vinh Cam (Petitioners).

Mr. Chow confirmed for the Court that he is appearing today to represent Muoi Nam Au and Phat Vinh Cam. The Court stated that it was in receipt of the joint case management statement which reported that the parties have reached a negotiated resolution and that the civil action will proceed. Mr. Jew stated that his clients are withdrawing their objections and that Mr. Cam's petitions are being withdrawn. Mr. Jew confirmed that there has been no formal dismissal. The Court confirmed that Ms. Su's petition is the only petition going forward. Mr. Chow requested that his client's petitions be taken off calendar/withdrawn. The Court stated that it did not have a proposed order and that it would not take the matter under submission today.

The Court ordered the following:

The Order to Show Cause is discharged;
The Status on Mediation is off calendar;
Muoi Nam Au (a.k.a. Muoi Nam Au) and Phat Vinh Cam's Petition for Order Confirming Title to Real Property is off calendar;
Muoi Nam Au (a.k.a. Muoi Nam Au) and Phat Vinh Cam's Amended Petition for Letters of Administration is off calendar;
The next Case Management Conference of April 5, 2023, is vacated;
The Trial date of May 31, 2023, is vacated;
Sang Au Su's Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed is continued to February 8, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 for receipt of the proposed order. Mr. Chow is to submit the proposed order by January 27, 2023.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Singh. Matter not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-05-2022 Continued To Jan-11-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jaynry Mak, via Zoom, for and with Phat V. Cam and Nam M. Au, Petitioners; Christopher Jew, via Zoom, for and with Sang A. Su, Mimi Su, and Hing Su, Respondents.

The court queries counsel as to the status of mediation. Counsel report that they have agreed to mediate on Dec-08-2022 and the mediation will cover only the probate matters and not the civil matter regarding the one-third interest in real property which is not an asset of the estate. Ms. Mak points the court to the Order to Show Cause (OSC) RE sanctions for failure to comply with the court's mediation order. Mr. Jew reports that he has filed a response to the OSC. The court advises that it will not impose sanctions at this time but intends to continue the OSC which will apply to both parties if either prevents the mediation from proceeding as scheduled.

All subject matters are continued to Jan-11-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status of Mediation is added for the same date. Notice is waived by counsel.

Judge: Hon. William Barry. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Saephan. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jul-27-2022 Continued To Oct-05-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-27-2022 Continued To Oct-05-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jaynry Mak, via Zoom, for Nam M. Au, and Phat V. Cam, Petitioners; Christopher Jew, via Zoom, for Sang A. Su, Objector.

The court advises counsel that it is not pleased that the parties did not comply with the Minute Order Referring the Case to Pro Bono Mediation issued by the court on Mar-24-2022 and queries counsel regarding the non-compliance. Mr. Jew offers testimony regarding the preceding. Ms. Mak advises that her clients were ready to mediate and of ongoing attempts to meet and confer and of the failure of the objector to confirm what exactly their demands are. The court states that mediation might have been productive regardless of the civil action and that now it appears that judicial resources, as well as Ms. Mak's and the mediator's time have been wasted on the ordering and setting of the mediation. The court advises that it would have issued a case management order at the previous calling of this matter but did not as the parties agreed to mediation. The court confirms the sanctions that Ms. Mak might be requesting based on the time her client and her spent preparing for mediation. The court advises that it will be issuing a Case Management Order and instructs Ms. Mak to monitor the court's register of actions to obtain a copy of the order and to serve it on all parties who are entitled to notice.

After additional discussion, the Status of Mediation taken off calendar. The competing Petitions for Letters of Administration, and Petition for Order Confirming Title, etc., are continued to Oct-05-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. An Order to Show Cause (OSC) as to why the court should not impose sanctions of up to $2500 for Objector Sang Au Su's failure to mediate is returnable on the same date. Mr. Jew is instructed to file a response to the OSC by noon on Sep-22-2022 and to deliver courtesy copies of the response by close of business on the same date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

Off Calendar Mar-23-2022 Continued To Jul-13-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Chow, via zoom, for Muoi Nam Au, and Phat Vinh Cam, Petitioners; Christopher Jew, via zoom, for Sang Au Su, Mini Su, and Hing Yip Su, Objectors.

The court confirms with counsel of their diligent attempts to meet and confer to help resolve this matter. The court strongly encourages counsel to discuss settlement to save costs associated with going to trial. Mr. Chow advises that he and Mr. Jew have agreed to request a continuance of 120 days to continue to meet and confer. The court suggests the parties consider utilizing the court's pro bono mediation program. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than Jun-24-2022. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by Jul-05-2022. The matters on today's calendar are continued to Jul-13-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The Parties are to return on the same date for status of mediation. John Chow is instructed to monitor the court's website to obtain a copy of the Minute Order Referring Case to Pro Bono Mediation and serve it on all parties who are entitled to notice.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.

Off Calendar Dec-29-2021 Continued To Mar-23-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-29-2021 Continued To Mar-23-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Jew appeared by zoom for and with Mimi Su (respondent) by zoom, for and with Hing Yip Su (respondent) by zoom and for and with Sang Au Su (petitioner/respondent) by zoom.
Jason Louie and Robert Chan appeared by zoom for and with Muoi Nam Au by zoom and for and with Phat Vinh Cam by zoom.
Jaynry Mak appeared by zoom.

The court noted that a substitution of attorney and an opposition to the amended petition are in the filing queue. Ms. Mak stated that she will be substituting in for Jason Louie and for Robert Chan, but there is only one substitution in the filing queue. Mr. Chan stated that he will be signing the substitution of attorney. Mr. Louie stated that as of the last fourteen days both sides of this dispute have new attorneys, that the new attorneys are getting acclimated and that they will probably need a little more time. The court stated that it was very sorry and distressed to hear about the passing of Christina Chen. The court also noted that there are three petitions pending and questions whether Sang Au Su received the $150,000.00 some time ago at the time of settlement of the earlier matter. Mr. Chan stated that Sang Au Su received the $150,000.00 in his office ten years ago and that Sang Au Su signed a receipt. The court stated that it would like the new attorneys to think about how they are going to resolve all of this without a great deal of litigation noting that it's going to take a lot of money for the new attorneys who have now been brought in. Mr. Jew stated that he has met with Mr. Chow and Ms. Mak, that they had discussion on how to try and resolve this matter, that they need time to get caught up and that if they can't work it out amongst themselves, they may ask for help from the court. Discussion was held regarding the possibility of a referral to pro bono mediation and the availability of the court's judicial mediation program. The court encourages the parties to make a best effort to come to resolution. The Petitions for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed (2), and the Petition for Order Confirming Title to Real Property are continued to March 23, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Order Confirming Title To Real Property
Off Calendar Oct-18-2021 Continued To Nov-17-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jason Louie, via ZOOM, for Phat Vinh Cam, via ZOOM, and Muoi Nam Au, Petitioners; Sang Au Su, in person, Petitioner.

Mr. Louie reports that opposing counsel, Christina Chen, has recently died. Mr. Su's son, Hing Su, confirms the preceding. The court queries Hing Su if his father plans to seek new counsel. Mr. Su advises that the plan is to seek counsel for a related civil matter but not this matter. The court advises Mr. Su that it would be in his father's best interest to hire new counsel for this matter and that an official notice of Ms. Chen's death will need to be filed with the court. The court then confirms with Mr. Su that his father plans to object to the petition on calendar today. Mr. Louie advises that related petitions in this matter are set for Nov-17-2021. The court advises Mr. Su that a formal written objection will need to be filed with the court and recommends that he seek legal assistance. The court then advises the parties that this matter will be continued to Nov-17-2021 to be heard with the related petitions and advises Mr. Su that any objections should be filed with the court and served on all parties by Nov-05-2021. The court instructs Mr. Louie to file a response to any objections by Nov-12-2021.

The Petition for Order Confirming, etc., is continued to Nov-17-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: C. Gomez. Not reported.
